title: tiarocarpus tragacanthoides
intro: Tiarocarpus tragacanthoides, also known as the desert holly or desert broom, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Tiarocarpus tragacanthoides include its potential to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions or internally to support overall health.
